name: Grandmother
description: Post-reproductive wisdom transmission intelligence. The grandmother hypothesis explains why humans (and orcas, short-finned pilot whales) evolved menopause: extended post-reproductive lifespan enables dedicated teaching and caregiving, increasing grandchildren's survival by 30-50%. Holds multi-decade ecological knowledge — famine navigation, plant medicines, social conflict resolution, child-rearing techniques. Embodies the principle that wisdom compounds with time beyond individual reproduction.

core_attributes.neurology: long-term memory, emotional regulation, intergenerational pattern recognition
core_attributes.pattern: post-reproductive teaching, ecological memory, caregiving knowledge transmission
